text: Using the DaVinci XI robotic system, the surgeon performs laparoscopic exploration with the patient in a supine Trendelenburg position. A small bowel loop is visibly incarcerated into the femoral canal on the right side.

Three 8mm robotic trocars are inserted, with the robotic system positioned on the patient's left side. The bedside surgeon is visible standing on the patient's right side, and no assistant trocar is present.

The surgeon performs a peritoneal incision between the medial umbilical ligament and the right anterior superior iliac spine, creating a peritoneal flap. Dissection of the angel's hair is visible, followed by dissection of the preparitoneal area using monopolar core re-mounted robotic scissors. The hernia sac is dissected medial to the epigastric vessels.
The surgeon performs an incision medial to the femoral ring in the lacunar ligament to reduce the incarcerated bowel loop, visibly freeing the loop from the femoral canal.

The surgeon completes the reduction of the hernia sac, revealing the right round ligament and demonstrating the femoral ring. Cyanine green dye is administered intravenously to assess bowel viability, with visible vascularization of the bowel observed.

A proline mesh is inserted into the preperitoneal space and fixed to the abdominal wall using cyanoacrylate glue. The surgeon then closes the peritoneal incision with a V-lock suture, completing the operation.
